name: Varunprasaath R. Selvaraju, Ph.D.
summary: PhD Chemist with 6+ years of experience in analytical method development, materials characterization, and process optimization. Expertise in salt/polymorph screening, solid-state analytics, thermal analysis (TGA/DSC), spectroscopy (FT-IR, UV-Vis), and chromatographic methods (HPLC, GPC/SEC). Strong background in DOE-driven analytical development and establishing structure-property relationships. Experienced in developing robust analytical methods from first principles for API and drug product characterization.

education.details: Dissertation: Modular Design and Electrochemical Properties of Xanthene-Based Electrochromic Materials

education.degree: B.S. (Research), Major: Chemistry; Minor: Materials Science

bullets: Develop and optimize analytical methods for polymer and nanoparticle characterization using HPLC, GPC/SEC, thermal analysis, and spectroscopy.
Apply DOE approaches to systematically characterize materials and establish optimal processing parameters.
Conduct solid-state characterization including thermal analysis (TGA/DSC) and spectroscopic analysis (FT-IR, UV-Vis) for phase identification and stability assessment.

experience.role: Graduate Researcher — Analytical Chemistry & Materials Characterization
bullets: Developed and validated analytical methods for small molecule characterization using HPLC, NMR, GC-MS, and spectroscopic techniques.
Performed comprehensive solid-state characterization to identify polymorphic forms and establish degradation pathways.
Applied statistical methods (DOE, multivariate analysis) to optimize analytical methods and process conditions.
Authored technical reports and presented findings at departmental reviews and scientific conferences.

skills.Analytical Methods: HPLC/UPLC, GPC/SEC, GC-MS, NMR, method development & validation
skills.Solid-State Analysis: TGA, DSC, FT-IR, UV-Vis, polymorph screening, PXRD concepts
skills.Data Analysis: DOE, SPC, multivariate analysis, Origin, JMP, MestReNova
skills.Characterization: Thermal analysis, spectroscopy, particle sizing, microscopy
